cards.name: Chromatic Coffee
cards.address: Chromatic Coffee, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a locally roasted pour-over or espresso drink. Chromatic takes their coffee seriously, and you'll taste the difference.
cards.why: Cool morning, good coffee — this is exactly the right call.

cards.name: Community Yoga and Heartfulness Meditation
cards.address: 1780 Hillsdale Ave San Jose
cards.blurb: Stretch through yoga and guided meditation at Cambrian Library.
cards.why: A slow Saturday morning calls for something that actually slows you down.

cards.name: Back A Yard Caribbean Grill
cards.address: Back A Yard, San Jose
cards.blurb: Head downtown for the jerk chicken plate at Back A Yard. Bold flavors, generous portions, and one of the best lunch spots San Jose has going.
cards.why: You'll be walking out full and happy for under $20.

cards.name: Guadalupe River Park & Gardens
cards.address: Guadalupe River Park
cards.blurb: Walk it off along the Guadalupe River trail. The path winds through the middle of downtown and feels surprisingly removed from the city buzz.
cards.why: A cool, overcast afternoon is honestly perfect for a walk like this.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Dio Deka in Los Gatos. The Greek menu is impressive, the patio is beautiful, and this is the kind of meal worth dressing up slightly for.
cards.why: It's Saturday night and you earned a proper dinner.

cards.name: Peralta Adobe & Fallon House
cards.address: Peralta Adobe, Downtown San Jose
cards.blurb: Swing through the Peralta Adobe on your way back into downtown San Jose. Two historic buildings, 200 years of local history, and it won't cost you a thing.
cards.why: A laid-back evening closer that's actually interesting.

cards.name: Heroes
cards.address: 24 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the shelves at Heroes, a comic and pop culture bookstore on Campbell Ave with a solid mix of graphic novels, back issues, and collectibles — easy to lose an hour in here.
cards.why: It's a fun, low-key morning stop with zero pressure to buy anything.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Head to the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum for real mummies, hieroglyphics, and artifacts you genuinely wouldn't expect to find in San Jose. The planetarium and garden grounds are worth a lap too.
cards.why: It's one of the most underrated places in the whole South Bay and way more interesting than it sounds.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: San Pedro Square Market is the move for lunch — grab a taco from one vendor, maybe ramen or banh mi from another, and find a patio seat to eat in the sun. Treat it like a food hall crawl.
cards.why: No commitment to a single menu, which is exactly what you want after a museum.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: The Computer History Museum is worth every minute. The main Revolution exhibit takes you from room-sized computers to the iPhone era and somehow makes all of it feel wild. Go at your own pace.
cards.why: The 71-degree afternoon is perfect for an indoor stretch, and this exhibit is genuinely hard to rush through.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll the lakeside paths, watch the ducks, and unwind on the grass as the afternoon cools down.
cards.why: A peaceful outdoor wind-down between the museum and your evening massage fits the day's easy-going vibe perfectly.

item.title: Draft Active Transportation Plan Public Workshop
item.summary: Community workshop to review the public draft of the Mountain View Active Transportation Plan and share input on creating a safer, more connected network for walking, biking, and rolling.

copy.x: Tonight in Mountain View: the city wants your input on how people walk, bike, and roll around town. 6 PM at City Hall. If you've ever wished a crosswalk existed or a bike lane connected somewhere useful — this is the room to say it. https://collaborate.mountainview.gov/atp
copy.threads: Tonight in Mountain View, the city is holding a public workshop on its Active Transportation Plan — basically, how do we make walking, biking, and rolling around town safer and more connected? 6 PM at City Hall. If you have thoughts on a missing sidewalk, a sketchy intersection, or a bike route that goes nowhere useful, this is your moment. https://collaborate.mountainview.gov/atp #MountainView #ActiveTransportation #SouthBay

copy.instagram:
Tonight in Mountain View, the city is opening up its Active Transportation Plan for public input — and they genuinely want to hear from you. 🚶🚲

The workshop kicks off at 6 PM at Mountain View City Hall. The plan focuses on making it safer and easier to get around by foot, bike, or anything in between. If you've ever dodged a car in a crosswalk that felt too short, or wished a bike lane actually connected to something useful, this is where that frustration turns into something constructive.

Public workshops like this are how neighborhoods actually change. Show up, look at the draft plan, and tell them what's missing or what's working.

Details and more info at the link below 👇

tonight-pick.imagePrompt: Risograph screen print illustration in 4:5 portrait ratio. NO PEOPLE, NO TEXT. Grainy 3-color overprint (electric teal, burnt sienna, cream) with heavy halftone texture and registration shift. Dense editorial composition with wide margins; torn paper and washi tape strips creating jagged section boundaries throughout. Abstract fragmented game die and stacked card silhouettes woven into chaotic color fields and diagonal stripe patterns. Zine energy—elements colliding, intentional imperfect overlaps, organic torn edges. Bold graphic energy over polish.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Walk or bike the lakeside loop, watch the windsurfers set up on the water, and soak in the cool morning air before the day heats up.
cards.why: A quick, easy outdoor stretch right in Mountain View makes for a refreshing start before you head into a full day of culture and food.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through the galleries and don't skip the sculpture garden out back. The contemporary rotating exhibits are worth slowing down for.
cards.why: Free admission and genuinely good art makes this an easy yes on a Friday morning.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the creekside trail and shake off the wine a little. The path is flat, shaded in spots, and genuinely relaxing at this pace.
cards.why: A mellow walk after an afternoon winery is the right move before a big evening.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Pull up to the counter and order the brisket plate. Get the mac and cheese on the side. Eat all of it.
cards.why: This is some of the best BBQ in the South Bay and a Friday dinner here never disappoints.

cards.name: Candlelight: The Best of Hans Zimmer
cards.address: 101 Paseo De San Antonio, San Jose
cards.blurb: Listen to Hans Zimmer film scores by candlelight orchestra.
cards.why: It's a genuinely cool way to end a Friday night and the atmosphere in that room is something else.

cards.name: House of Bagels
cards.address: 1712-D Miramonte Ave, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at House of Bagels in Mountain View. Grab a fresh bagel with your spread of choice and a coffee while the day warms up.
cards.why: It's a classic South Bay breakfast move and they're open early.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hit The Dish Trail from the Stanford Ave trailhead. It's a steady climb with sweeping views of the Bay and foothills — worth pushing to the top before the afternoon crowd shows up.
cards.why: High 68°F is perfect hiking weather and the views from the ridge are genuinely great.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk through Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus. The Rodin sculptures in the outdoor garden are a highlight, and the rotating galleries inside are always worth a look.
cards.why: Free world-class art right on campus, and it's a natural next stop after the hike.

cards.name: Oren's Hummus
cards.address: 261 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Oren's Hummus on University Ave for lunch. Order the hummus plate, add a side or two, and eat well without overthinking it.
cards.why: It's one of the best lunch values on the Peninsula and you'll leave satisfied.

cards.name: Restaurant Gish
cards.address: 1998 Homestead Rd #113, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Drive over to Restaurant Gish in Santa Clara for dinner. The menu skews creative and the kitchen takes it seriously, so try the local favorite dishes and let the evening slow down.
cards.why: It punches above its weight for a Saturday night dinner.

cards.name: Art of Science 2026
cards.address: 475 Via Ortega, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Browse submissions celebrating the visual beauty of scientific research from campus departments.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

Tonight's the night if you've been sitting on that open mic idea 🎤

@sanjoseimprov hosts their monthly open mic on the second Wednesday of the month — and that's tonight. The show runs 8 to 9:30 PM, sign-ups open before the set, and the cost is easy on your wallet.

Maybe you perform. Maybe you just go to watch and cheer on strangers who are braver than you. Either way, it's a real good Wednesday evening in San Jose. ✨

tonight-pick.imagePrompt: Mid-century modern abstract illustration, 4:5 portrait ratio. NO PEOPLE, NO TEXT. Warm muted palette—burnt ochre, soft sage, cream, warm taupe—with organic flowing curves suggesting an abstracted microphone silhouette fragmented across composition. Dense layered design with deliberate geometric accents: sharp triangles, circles, small squares placed rhythmically throughout. Atomic-era atomic starburst shapes woven into color fields. Generous breathing room between elements. Restrained, editorial, sophisticated mood. Clean mid-mod linework paired with soft organic blob shapes creating sophisticated visual rhythm.

cards.name: Bill's Cafe
cards.address: Bill's Cafe, Willow Glen
cards.blurb: Start the morning right with the eggs benedict or a breakfast burrito at this Willow Glen institution. Settle into a booth and take your time.
cards.why: It's the kind of breakfast spot that actually makes you want to get out of bed.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk the paths through thousands of roses at peak spring bloom. Bring a coffee to go and just wander — it earns its reputation this time of year.
cards.why: Mid-May is about as good as it gets for seeing these roses.

cards.name: Santana Row Farmers Market
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose
cards.blurb: Shop for local produce, artisan goods, and ready-to-eat food weekly.
cards.why: Good food, open air, and zero commitment — just show up and graze.

cards.name: Gali Vineyards
cards.address: 21 W Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Pull up to Gali Vineyards for a glass on the patio when they open at 3. It's a low-key wine spot in downtown Los Gatos worth lingering at.
cards.why: A Wednesday afternoon with a glass of wine and nowhere to be is exactly the move.

cards.name: Central Park
cards.address: 909 Kiely Blvd, Santa Clara, CA 95051, USA
cards.blurb: Walk it off at Central Park in Santa Clara — a quick stretch of legs before the evening kicks in.
cards.why: Good reset between wine and dinner without overthinking it.

cards.name: Pacific Horizons: AAPI Mixer & AI 2.0 Workshop
cards.address: 88 S. 4th Street, 7th Floor,, San Jose,
cards.blurb: Network with Asian American Pacific Islander professionals and learn about AI innovations in a workshop.
cards.why: Free networking event with actual substance behind it — not just small talk and name tags.
cards.venue: Rotary Summit Center (Operated by Scott’s Seafood)

copy.instagram:
We planned your Wednesday — here's the whole day 🌹

7:30 AM — @billscafe, Willow Glen. Eggs benedict or a breakfast burrito. It's a neighborhood institution for a reason.

9:15 AM — San Jose Municipal Rose Garden. Thousands of roses at peak spring bloom. Bring your coffee to go and just wander.

11:00 AM — @santanarow Farmers Market. Local produce, artisan goods, and plenty of ready-to-eat things to snack on while you browse.

3:00 PM — @galivineyards. A low-key wine patio opening at 3 — exactly the kind of afternoon spot you want on a Wednesday.

5:00 PM — Central Park (@santaclaracitylibrary), Santa Clara. A quick walk to shake things out before the evening.

6:00 PM — Pacific Horizons: AAPI Mixer & AI 2.0 Workshop (@sj_downtown). Network with AAPI professionals and dig into what's happening with AI. A genuinely good way to end the day. 🤝

We built the full plan so you can just show up. Link in the details 👇

copy.email: Wednesday's lineup takes you from a leisurely eggs benedict at Bill's Cafe in Willow Glen to a peak-season stroll through the San Jose Municipal Rose Garden, then grazing and shopping at the Santana Row Farmers Market. The afternoon opens up at Gali Vineyards at 3, with a leg-stretch at Central Park in Santa Clara before the Pacific Horizons AAPI Mixer & AI 2.0 Workshop wraps the night — six stops, one great day.

DESIGN DIRECTION:
Concert flyer / xeroxed punk aesthetic — high-contrast black-and-white with photocopy grain, torn edges, ransom-note mixed-font typography, DIY energy.

COMPOSITION DIRECTION (interpret within the style above):
Layout: centered axis — all content anchored to a vertical center line, symmetrical feel, clean spine running through.
Typography: monospace typewriter-style face for times and metadata, a contrasting display face for venue names.
Decoration: sharp geometric accents — triangles, circles, small squares — placed deliberately for rhythm.
Density: generous breathing room around headlines and between stops.
Mood: playful and loud — embrace the chaos, let elements collide, energy over polish.

CRITICAL: All text must be spelled correctly and fully legible. This is a graphic design poster, NOT a photograph. The text content is the star — make it beautiful but readable. Do NOT include "STOP 1", "STOP 2" labels — just the times and venue names.
